company: Resonance Health Analysis Services Pty Ltd
intended_use: HepaFat-AI is intended for quantitative measurement of the triglyceride fat fraction in magnetic resonance images of the liver, also known as volumetric liver fat fraction (VLFF). It utilises magnetic
intended_use_full: HepaFat-AI is intended for quantitative measurement of the triglyceride fat fraction in magnetic resonance images of the liver, also known as volumetric liver fat fraction (VLFF). It utilises magnetic resonance images that exploit the difference in resonance frequencies between hydrogen nuclei in water and triglyceride fat. The quantitative triglyceride fat fraction is based on the measurement of a magnetic resonance parameter that reflects the ratio of the proton density signal of triglyceride fat to the total proton density signal in the liver. When interpreted by a trained physician, the results provide information that can aid in diagnosis.
target_condition: Confirmed or suspected fatty liver disease (NAFLD/NASH); monitoring liver fat content in weight loss management; assessment and screening of living donors for liver transplant
ground_truth: Liver biopsy with histological scoring of steatosis
other_metrics: Positive percent agreement (PPA) and negative percent agreement (NPA) reported as primary metrics; repeatability assessed; sensitivity and specificity reported at three VLFF thresholds: Grade 0 vs Grades 1-3 (Sensitivity 97.6%, Specificity 88.2%); Grades 0&1 vs Grades 2&3 (Sensitivity 86.1%, Specificity 74.8%); Grades 0-2 vs Grade 3 (Sensitivity 100.0%, Specificity 71.4%)
